title: Coryphantha pycnacantha
taxonomy: Coryphantha pycnacantha is classified as a species of cactus that belongs to the family Cactaceae. The genus Coryphantha includes various species of cacti, and C. pycnacantha is one of them. This species is native to the southwestern part of the United States and northern Mexico. It is a type of small to medium-sized cactus, known for its distinctive spines and flowers. C. pycnacantha is often found growing in dry, rocky areas.

intro: Coryphantha pycnacantha, also known as a type of prickly pear cactus, is a succulent plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including wounds, fever, and digestive issues, due to its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties.
